rationale: Ground truth is essentially empty (just LGTM/approvals), so both candidates trivially satisfy recall; the real differentiator is depth and usefulness of extra findings. X's source-code verification is rigorous but its only 'comment' is heavily hedged and non-actionable ('defer to your test', 'keep current wording either way'), amounting to a pure sanity-check rather than a concrete ask. Y did a repo-wide grep sweep and caught a genuine, grounded cross-file consistency gap (stale extra_body usage in examples/online_serving/minicpmo/README.md:58) with a concrete suggested fix, which is a real value-add beyond the diff; its two 'nit' wording critiques are grounded but low-value/pedantic, slightly denting precision. Net, Y is more thorough and actionable at a small cost to precision, giving it a slight edge.
